look_at.text: The way to the kitchen. Coffee, microwaves, passive-aggressive notes.
The way to the aisle corridor. The lab and elevator are through there.
The door to IT. A sign: 'Knock before entering. No, really. We mean it.'

open.text: You push open the IT department door.

use.text: You push open the IT department door.

look_at.text: Over-ear headphones, firmly on. The coworker is in another dimension entirely. A 'Property of Amazon' sticker is half-peeled off the left cup. The music is faintly audible. It sounds like lo-fi beats.

use.text: I should PICK UP the headphones if I want them.

look_at.text: A coworker deep in focus mode, headphones on, completely unreachable. They are in the zone. The zone has no doors.
A coworker without headphones, which means they are theoretically available for conversation. They look like they regret this.

talk_to.text: They can't hear a word. The headphones are a wall. A very comfortable, noise-cancelling wall.

pick_up.text: I'm not picking up a person.

give.text: They don't seem to want anything.

look_at.text: An empty desk, already cleared. A nameplate has been removed, leaving a clean rectangle in the dust. The absence is loud.

open.text: The drawer slides open. Empty, except for a pen lid, a dead AA battery, and a layer of crumbs that tell a story I don't want to know.

look_at.text: A cold coffee mug sitting at the empty desk. The owner isn't coming back for it.

pick_up.text: It's just a mug. I don't need a mug.

use.text: There's nothing useful to do with an abandoned coffee mug.

look_at.text: A whiteboard covered in the residue of several months of meetings. Visible text: 'OKRs', 'Key Inputs', 'Dive Deep', 'Disagree & Commit', 'Customer Obsession', and at the bottom in a different colour: 'What are we even doing here?' Nobody has erased that last one.

use.text: I'm not writing anything. This is someone else's strategic vision.

pick_up.text: It's fixed to the wall. The corporate wisdom is permanent.

look_at.text: 'THINK OUTSIDE THE BOX — but stay inside the building.' Someone has added in pen: '...and inside the process, and inside the policy, and inside the approved communication channels.' The additions continue off the edge of the poster.

pick_up.text: It's screwed to the wall. Like Alex.

look_at.text: A large printer-copier. The display reads 'READY' but there's a Post-it on it that says 'DO NOT USE — Chip (IT)'. The Post-it has been there long enough to curl at the corners.

use.text: The printer hums ominously. A Post-it from IT says not to touch it. I'll leave it alone.

open.text: I open the paper tray. Empty. I close it again. That's all I needed to know.

look_at.text: An open cupboard. Old printer cartridges, a fire warden vest that has never been worn. You've already taken the book.

An open cupboard. Old printer cartridges, a fire warden vest that has never been worn, and one book, spine-out: 'WINNING UGLY: Psychological Tactics for the Desperately Outmatched.' You pocket it immediately.

use.text: It's already open. Nothing else to do with it.
